revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大将軍神社 (奈良市)]]


{{Infobox religious building
|名称   = Daishōgun Shrine (大将軍神社)
|image  = File:Daishogun Jinja (Nara) Torii.jpg
|所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}} Shijo Avenue ３丁目４
|祭神   = {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|qid=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|1=Iwanaga-hime}}
|社格   = Former Village Shrine{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=28|qq=}}
|創建   = 
|本殿   = Kasuga Taisha subsidiary shrine main hall (Enomoto Shrine old main hall transfer theory){{sfn|奈良県史|p=910|qq=}}
|例祭   = October 10
|文化財 = 
|ISO    = JP-29
|緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 52.08
|経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 43.15
|地図   = Nara City
|prefecture= {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}
|coordinates= {{Coord|34.6811333|N|135.7953194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
}}
'''Daishōgun Shrine''' (だいしょうぐんじんじゃ) is a shrine located in 3-chōme, Shijo Avenue, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It was relocated from {{ill|Saionji Village|ja|斎音寺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=309|qq=}}.
== {{nihongo|Worshipped deities|祭神|Saijin|qq=}} ==
* {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|qid=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|1=Iwanaga-hime}}
== Shrine facilities ==
=== Main Hall ===
There is a theory that the main hall was transferred from the old main hall of the Enomoto Shrine, an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|qid=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Taisha|1=Kasuga-taisha}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=910|qq=}}.
=== Sub-shrines ===
* Ōmiya Shrine – The enshrined deity is {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Ohirume no Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=309|qq=}}. It is located on the left (west) side of the main hall{{sfn|奈良市史|p=309|qq=}}.
== Festivals ==
Although conducted by a council of six, the annual festival is held by welcoming priests from {{ill|Yamatokōriyama|en|Yamatokōriyama|qid=Q118213|lt=Yamato-Koriyama|1=Yamatokōriyama}}’s {{ill|Yanagisawa Shrine|ja|柳沢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagisawa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=309|qq=}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist|qq=}}
== Bibliography ==

{{osm box|n|5300440888|qq=}}

* {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史|qq=}}|qq=}}

{{Iwanaga Faith|qq=}}
